meta.title: A209 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes CLK A209 engine — M271, M112, M272, M113, M273, M156, OM646, OM612, OM642. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLK A209 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned A209 engine typically costs £2,200–£14,000+ supply only, depending on the engine — the volume OM646 diesel and M112 V6 sit at £2,500–£5,500, while the M156 AMG unit ranges from £8,000–£14,000+. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes CLK A209 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M112.953 V6 (CLK 320) and OM646.963 diesel (CLK 220 CDI) are the most dependable. The M112 is a bulletproof naturally aspirated V6 with no balance-shaft issues and a 200,000-mile-plus lifespan. The OM646 is a refined, proven common-rail diesel with excellent longevity. The M113 V8 is also extremely robust.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLK A209?

acceptedAnswer.text: M271 timing chain stretch and supercharger clutch failure (CLK 200 Kompressor), M272/M273 balance shaft failure on pre-September 2006 engines, M272/M273 intake swirl flap failure, OM612 injector and fuel pump issues (CLK 270 CDI), and — unrelated to the engine — hydraulic roof system leaks on the cabriolet.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLK A209 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The M112 V6, M113 V8, OM646 diesel and OM642 V6 diesel are genuinely robust and well-documented. The M271 Kompressor has known timing chain and supercharger issues. The M272 and M273 have balance shaft issues on pre-2006 engines. The M156 AMG is reliable for a 500bhp+ engine but expensive to maintain.

mainEntity.name: Which A209 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but the M271.940/955 (CLK 200 Kompressor) is the one we'd advise caution on. The timing chain and supercharger clutch issues are well-documented and expensive to fix. If you buy one, ensure the chain and tensioner have been replaced. Pre-2006 M272/M273 engines also need balance shaft history checked.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my A209 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain rattle or loss of boost (M271), check engine light with timing codes (M272/M273 balance shaft), rough idle or diesel smell (OM646/OM612 injector failure), or oil leaks from the valve covers (M113 V8) are key warnings. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes CLK A209 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £8,000+ A209 (especially a V8 or AMG), a £3,000–£4,500 recon is 38–56% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £3,000 example with rust or roof issues, a £3,000+ recon may exceed the car's worth — walk away. Our 70% Mercedes threshold is the line. For the M156 AMG, the maths is different — if you can afford a £15,000+ CLK 63, you can afford the maintenance.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes CLK A209 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M112 V6, M113 V8 and OM646 diesel can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing. The M272/M273 are typically good for 150,000+ miles if the balance shaft has been addressed. The M271 is the shortest-lived due to its timing chain and supercharger issues. The M156 AMG is typically good for 80,000–120,000 miles if maintained to AMG standards.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the A209 and C209?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both are the second-generation CLK-Class. The A209 is the 2-door cabriolet (convertible, 2002–2009), while the C209 is the 2-door coupé (2001–2008). They share the same platform, engines, and most components — but the cabriolet has the hydraulic roof system and additional structural reinforcements. Costs vary slightly due to the roof system — see the C209 generation page for specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLK A209 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the block. Tell us your variant (e.g., CLK 320, CLK 220 CDI, CLK 500) and registration year, and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes CLK A209 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the CLK A209 cabriolet — from the M271 Kompressor four-cylinders to the M112 and M272 V6 petrols, the M113 and M156 V8s, and the OM646, OM612 and OM642 diesels. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the balance-shaft and swirl-flap issues that define this generation's ownership story. Backed by 252+ real UK CLK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes CLK A209 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the CLK A209 cabriolet specifically — from the entry-level four-cylinder Kompressor to the fire-breathing M156 V8 in the CLK 63 AMG.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: M112 V6 and M113 V8 families: 85% confidence — among the most durable Mercedes engines ever built, with a well-documented reputation for reliability when maintained . OM646: 80% confidence — proven common-rail diesel with strong longevity . OM642: 80% confidence — robust V6 diesel with good reputation. M271: 75% confidence — known for timing chain and supercharger issues . M272 and M273: 75% confidence — balance shaft issues affect early units (pre-September 2006) . M156: 75% confidence — high-performance AMG V8 with higher running costs but good reliability for a 500bhp+ engine.

overview.intro: The A209 is the convertible body style of the second-generation CLK-Class, launched in February 2002 as the successor to the A208 cabriolet . Based on the Mercedes W203 C-Class platform, it uses rack-and-pinion steering instead of the recirculating-ball design of the previous generation . The engine range evolved significantly: early cars offered M112 V6 petrols, M113 V8s, and OM612 five-cylinder diesels . A 2005 facelift brought revised styling, the M272 and M273 V6/V8 petrols, and the OM646 and OM642 diesels . The range-topping CLK 63 AMG, introduced in 2006, used the M156 6.2-litre V8 producing 474bhp . The A209 was the last complete car built by Karmann before the facility closed .
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes W203 (shared with C-Class) • Predecessor: A208 • Successor: A207 E-Class Cabriolet • Sibling body style in this generation: C209 Coupé.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The CLK A209 generated 252 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED], concentrated on the M112/M272 V6 petrols and OM646 diesel as these cars enter enthusiast-owner territory.

items.quote: The 2.1-litre OM646 is the most refined and reliable diesel in the A209 range. It replaced the less-reliable OM612 five-cylinder and offers 148bhp with a genuine 200,000-mile-plus lifespan when properly maintained . Injector seal 'Black Death' is the only real concern — and it's easily preventable.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy without the OM612's five-cylinder quirks.

items.quote: The 3.2-litre M112 V6 is arguably the most bulletproof engine ever fitted to a CLK. 218bhp of smooth, naturally aspirated power, no balance-shaft issues, no timing-chain worries — just a robust, proven V6 that keeps going. The 2.6-litre M112.912 in the CLK 240 is equally solid .
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want V6 smoothness without the M272's balance-shaft risk.

items.quote: The 1.8-litre supercharged M271 is the A209's entry-level petrol — but it comes with known issues: timing chain stretch, camshaft adjuster wear, and supercharger clutch failure . Repair costs can quickly exceed the value of a budget CLK. If you buy one, ensure the chain and tensioner have been replaced.
items.whoItsFor: budget-conscious buyers willing to accept higher maintenance costs.

items.quote: The 6.2-litre M156 V8 is a masterpiece — 474bhp of naturally aspirated AMG thunder. But when it fails, it fails expensively. Camshaft adjusters, lifters, and the sheer cost of AMG-specific parts mean replacement bills frequently hit five figures. The Black Series M156.983 is even more costly.
items.whoItsFor: collectors and enthusiasts with deep pockets.

items.quote: Often overlooked in favour of the more powerful CLK 320, the 2.6-litre M112 offers 170bhp, the same bulletproof reliability, and significantly better fuel economy. It's cheaper to buy, cheaper to insure, and cheaper to run — the sweet spot in the A209 petrol range.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want V6 refinement without the running costs of a larger engine.

items.quote: The 3.0-litre OM642 V6 diesel is rare in the A209 but offers 224bhp and 510Nm of torque — more than the CLK 500 V8 in pulling power . It's a refined, powerful diesel that transforms the CLK into a genuine grand tourer. The OM642 is well-regarded for reliability, though early units can have oil cooler seal issues.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel torque without the four-cylinder's limitations.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — bulletproof V6, no major issues.
Facelift V6 — great engine, but check balance shaft.
AMG V8 — reliable, but expensive when it fails.
Most Expensive — AMG masterpiece, AMG bills.

economicsRule.title: The A209 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,500–£4,500 OM646 recon against a £5,000 A209 sits at 50–90% of the car's value. On a sub-£4,000 CLK, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £8,000+ example (particularly a V8 or AMG), the same recon drops to a routine 31–56% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first. For the M156 AMG, the maths is different — if you can afford a £15,000+ CLK 63, you can afford the maintenance.

problems.title: M271 Timing Chain & Supercharger Clutch Failure
problems.rootCause: The M271's timing chain tensioner can fail, leading to chain stretch and potentially catastrophic valve damage . The supercharger clutch is also a known weak point — failure causes loss of boost and a rattling noise . Camshaft adjuster wear is another common issue.
problems.recommendation: On any M271-powered A209 over 60,000 miles, have the timing chain tensioner and supercharger clutch inspected at every service — £600–£1,200 now is far cheaper than the £2,200–£4,000+ replacement that follows a skipped chain.

cta.label: Compare M271 engine prices for your CLK 200 Kompressor →

problems.title: M272/M273 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.affectedModels: CLK 280, CLK 350, CLK 550 (M272/M273 V6/V8 engines, pre-September 2006).
problems.rootCause: The M272 and M273 engines have a well-documented balance shaft and idler gear issue that causes the timing to jump, leading to engine damage . This affects engines built before August 2006 . Post-2006 engines have the revised parts and are much more reliable.
problems.recommendation: If you're buying a pre-2006 CLK 280, 350 or 550, check the engine number against Mercedes' affected range. If it's affected, budget for balance shaft replacement or walk away. A £1,000–£1,800 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £3,500–£6,500 replacement that follows a complete failure.

cta.label: Compare M272/M273 engine prices for your CLK 280/350/550 →

problems.title: M272/M273 Intake Manifold Swirl Flap Failure
problems.rootCause: The M272 and M273 intake manifolds have adjustable air flaps (swirl flaps) that can break, causing poor idle, loss of power, and check engine lights . This is a common issue across Mercedes V6 and V8 engines of this era.
problems.recommendation: If your M272 or M273 develops a rough idle or check engine light with swirl-flap-related codes, have the intake manifold inspected immediately — £500–£900 now is far cheaper than the £1,500–£2,500 replacement that follows a complete failure.

cta.label: Compare M272/M273 intake manifold repair quotes →

problems.title: OM612 Injector & Fuel Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The OM612 five-cylinder diesel can suffer from injector failure, high-pressure fuel pump issues, and turbocharger problems . The injectors are prone to 'Black Death' seal failure, and the fuel pump's quantity control valve is a known weak point.
problems.recommendation: The OM612 is less refined than the later OM646 — if you're buying a CLK 270 CDI, budget for injector and fuel pump inspection. A £500–£900 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £3,000–£5,000 replacement that follows a catastrophic failure.

cta.label: Compare OM612 engine prices for your CLK 270 CDI →

problems.title: A209 Hydraulic Roof System Leaks
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage, more common with age.
problems.rootCause: Not an engine failure, but the single most common non-engine issue on the A209 — the convertible roof's hydraulic system can develop leaks in the lines, cylinders, or pump . This is a complex, expensive system to repair.
problems.recommendation: Have the hydraulic roof system inspected on any A209 — a £200–£500 inspection is far cheaper than the £1,500–£3,000+ replacement that follows a complete system failure. Check for fluid leaks in the boot area where the pump is located.

cta.label: Compare A209 roof hydraulic repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: A209 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Model Page's baseline figures — A209 cabriolet shares its W203-derived engine bay with the C209 coupé, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required. M156 AMG costs reflect AMG-specific parts and specialist labour requirements.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are A209 cabriolet only. C209 Coupé shares most of these engines but is covered on its own Generation Page, as fitment and labour access vary slightly by body style.
